description: Add stress tests for MCP server tool dispatch: concurrent calls, dispatch during tab close/navigation, hot reload during active dispatch, permission changes mid-dispatch. These test the MCP server directly via createMcpClient without side panel.

userStories.title: Concurrent tool dispatch stress test (10+ parallel calls to same tab)
userStories.description: Add stress test that fires 10+ tool calls concurrently via Promise.all to the same plugin tab and verifies all complete without interleaving, corruption, or dropped results.
acceptanceCriteria: Test fires 10 concurrent e2e-test_echo calls with unique messages via Promise.all
All 10 results are non-error and contain the correct echo response matching their input
No two results contain the same message (no response routing corruption)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts (NEW file)

Context:
The dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts file already tests 5 concurrent calls, but we need higher concurrency and verification that response routing is correct. The e2e-test plugin's echo tool returns `{ message: <input> }` so we can verify each response matches its input.

Changes:
Create new file with manual setup pattern: createTestConfigDir, writeTestConfig with e2e-test plugin at auto+reviewed, startMcpServer, startTestServer, launchExtensionContext, setupAdapterSymlink. Open matching tab via openTestAppTab. Wait for extension connected + tab.syncAll. Create MCP client, initialize.

Test: Fire 10 calls `Promise.all(Array.from({length: 10}, (_, i) => mcpClient.callTool('e2e-test_echo', { message: `concurrent-${i}` })))`. Verify all results are non-error. Parse each result JSON and verify `message` field matches the input. Collect all messages into a Set and verify Set.size === 10 (no duplicates).

Then fire 20 calls to verify higher concurrency. Same verification.

Constraints:
- Must have a matching tab open (test server running) for plugin dispatch to work
- Use OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS=1 to avoid permission dialogs
- Follow existing manual setup/teardown pattern from dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts

userStories.title: Tool dispatch during tab close
userStories.description: Start a slow tool call, close the matching tab mid-execution, verify the tool returns a clean error (not a 30s timeout hang).
acceptanceCriteria: Test starts a slow_with_progress tool call (3s duration), closes the matching tab after 500ms
Tool call resolves with isError: true within 10s (not the full 30s dispatch timeout)
Error message indicates tab closure or dispatch failure, not a generic timeout
Server remains healthy after the failed dispatch (health endpoint returns ok)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts (add to same file)

Context:
The e2e-test plugin has a `slow_with_progress` tool that takes `durationMs` and `steps` params. When the tab closes mid-execution, the extension should detect it and send an error response back through the dispatch chain. The dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts file already tests 'tool call during extension reconnect window' but not tab closure specifically.

Changes:
Add test that: opens matching tab, starts slow_with_progress with durationMs=3000. After 500ms (via setTimeout + Promise race), closes the tab page. The tool call should resolve with isError=true. Verify the error content mentions the tab or dispatch failure. Then verify server.health() still returns status='ok'.

Constraints:
- Use Promise.race or separate Promise for the tab close timing
- The e2e-test plugin's slow_with_progress tool signature: { durationMs: number, steps: number }
- Do NOT use page.close() on the side panel — close the TEST APP tab

userStories.title: Hot reload during active tool dispatch
userStories.description: Start tool calls, trigger hot reload mid-execution, verify calls either complete or fail cleanly (no hang, no crash).
acceptanceCriteria: Test starts 5 slow tool calls (2s each), triggers server.triggerHotReload() after 500ms
All 5 calls resolve within 30s (no infinite hang)
Each call is either successful or returns isError with a meaningful error
After hot reload completes, new tool calls succeed normally
Server health returns ok after reload
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts (add to same file)

Context:
Hot reload (SIGUSR1 to dev proxy) kills the worker process and forks a new one. In-flight tool calls go through the extension WebSocket which disconnects and reconnects. The dev-proxy buffers HTTP requests during restart, but WebSocket messages in flight may be lost.

Changes:
Start 5 slow_with_progress calls (durationMs=2000, steps=2) via Promise.allSettled (not Promise.all — some may fail). After 500ms, call server.triggerHotReload(). Wait for all calls to settle. Verify none are stuck (all resolved/rejected). Count successes and failures. Then wait for 'Hot reload complete' in server logs. Make a fresh tool call to verify the server is back.

Constraints:
- Use Promise.allSettled, not Promise.all (some calls WILL fail during reload)
- Must wait for 'Hot reload complete' log before making verification call
- The MCP client may need to re-initialize after reload (session lost)

userStories.title: Permission change mid-dispatch
userStories.description: Start a tool call with permission 'auto', change permission to 'off' mid-execution via config, verify the in-flight call completes but subsequent calls are rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: Test starts a slow tool call (3s), changes tool permission to 'off' via config + POST /reload after 500ms
The in-flight call completes (either success or clean error)
A subsequent call to the same tool returns isError with 'disabled' or 'not been reviewed' message
Changing permission back to 'auto' restores tool availability
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts (add to same file)

Context:
Permission changes propagate via config.json write + POST /reload or config watcher. The MCP server checks permission at dispatch time. An in-flight call that already passed the permission check should complete; the permission change only affects new calls.

Changes:
Setup with e2e-test at 'auto'. Start slow_with_progress (3s). After 500ms, write config changing e2e-test permission to 'off', then POST /reload. Wait for the slow call to settle. Then try calling e2e-test_echo — should fail with disabled message. Write config back to 'auto', POST /reload, verify echo works again.

Constraints:
- Must use writeTestConfig + POST /reload (not side panel) to change permission
- The POST /reload helper: fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/reload`, { method: 'POST', headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${secret}` } })
- Use readTestConfig to verify config persistence
